title: تنفيذ سياسات الصحة النفسية للأطفال والمراهقين
reference_info: WHO — Child and Adolescent Mental Health Policies
text_original: health policy. This is more likely to be the case if Steps 1 to 5 each other in a synergistic have been followed. Second, there needs to be a high degree of intersectoral and manner. interagency collaboration, so that opportunities for synergism can be grasped and unnecessary duplication avoided. Clearly, this is more likely if the stakeholder consultation process has been inclusive and exhaustive, and if participation has been Box 6 identifies some common elements from experiences of implementing policies in several parts of the world over the past few years. Box 6. Areas for action for child and adolescent mental health Intersectoral collaboration Legislation and human rights Information systems Research and evaluation of policies and services Quality improvement Organization of services Promotion, prevention, treatment and rehabilitation Improving access to and use of psychotropic medicines Human resources development and training While there is general consensus that ministries of health need to take action in most of these areas in order to meet the overall objectives of a health policy, the emphasis given to each area, or the priorities within each area, will vary from one country/region to another. The mental health needs and demands of the population, the societal organization and culture, as well as public policies will determine the emphasis or priorities in each of these areas. Adequate and sustained financing is one of the most important factors in the implementation of policy. It is a powerful tool that allows the ministry of health to Adequate and sustained transform
text_simplified: يشرح النص أهمية التعاون بين القطاعات المختلفة والوكالات لتنفيذ سياسات الصحة النفسية للأطفال والمراهقين. يشمل ذلك مجالات مثل التشريعات وحقوق الإنسان وأنظمة المعلومات وتحسين الخدمات. كما يؤكد على ضرورة التمويل المستدام كعامل أساسي لنجاح السياسات.
